//: release-please derives the version from Conventional Commit messages instead of the old release:* PR-label fold (see .github/workflows/release-please.yml). We squash-merge, so the squashed commit message is the PR title, which .github/workflows/pr-title.yml lints as a Conventional Commit. feat: bumps minor, fix:/perf: bump patch, and a ! or BREAKING CHANGE bumps major; chore:/docs:/ci:/etc. no longer bump the number but are still recorded in the changelog via changelog-sections below.
//bootstrap-sha: The repo has 270 merged PRs of label-era history but no tags. bootstrap-sha marks the last commit release-please should ignore, so the first release PR only considers commits merged AFTER it — it does not replay the entire label-era history into one giant changelog. This is PR #509, the last commit whose release:* label fold is banked into the seeded 17.11.3 in .release-please-manifest.json (and version.txt), so numbering continues unbroken. (PR #510 merged just after; its squash message is not a Conventional Commit, so release-please ignores it — no bump, no changelog noise.)

..//changelog-sections: Only feat/fix/perf trigger a version bump, but we list every commit type here so the CHANGELOG still records internal-only changes — preserving the old scheme's 'every merge is visible' property even though chore no longer moves the number.
